methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Durin is a pre-seed stage infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $3.4M to date. The comparable universe in this sector is sparse: two private peers (AiDash and Watoga Technologies) exist, but neither provides sufficient data for a reliable peer-median approach due to stage and valuation-tier misalignment. No public comparables were identified in the sector.

Given the lack of narrow peer coverage, stage-progression methodology is the only applicable triangulation approach. Using a $12.0B total-raised proxy as a starting point and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for pre-seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $30.0B. This method assumes the proxy base case and step-up cadence are reasonable for frontier infrastructure plays.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $25.5B to $34.5B (median $30.0B), with that single method driving the range. The primary uncertainty stems from the absence of market comparables; the valuation rests entirely on stage-progression mechanics rather than peer triangulation. Before relying on this range for decision-making, validation against founder guidance, investor market expectations, or comparable infrastructure-stage benchmarks outside this dataset would be prudent.
